title: Plot-Induced Stupidity
wikitext:
{{Policies and Explanations Pages}}
==Description==
'''Plot-Induced Stupidity''', or '''PIS''' for short, is a term used to refer to events in a story that contradict a character's normal capabilities for the purpose of the plot. For example, if a generally competent, intelligent villain does something stupid like not killing the hero immediately when he has the chance, and that allows the hero to win, that would be an example of PIS (If the villain was just incompetent in general, it would be '''CIS'''/'''[[Character-Induced Stupidity]]''' instead).

===Examples===
*[[Hulk (Marvel Comics)|Hulk]] being defeated by [[Captain America (Marvel Comics)|Captain America]], and an ordinary python, or being severely harmed by regular gorillas.
*[[Thor (Marvel Comics)|Thor]] being knocked unconscious by an ordinary gunshot.
*[[Superboy-Prime]] being able to harm [[Mister Mxyzptlk]], and being defeated himself by the Teen Titans.
*[[Dormammu]] being harmed by [[Cyclops (Marvel Comics)|Cyclops]], and defeated by [[Magik (Marvel Comics)|Illyana Rasputina]], or Frankenstein's Monster.
*[[Spider-Man (Marvel Comics)|Spider-Man]] beating [[Firelord]].
*[[Arceus]] losing its Plates and nearly dying after stopping a meteor.
*[[Black Panther (Marvel Comics)|Black Panther]] subduing the [[Silver Surfer (Marvel Comics)|Silver Surfer]].
*[[Gambit]] beating [[Gladiator (Marvel Comics)|Gladiator]].
*[[She-Hulk]] almost being killed by a very small explosion.
*[[Zack Fair]] being killed by bullets.
